content: Just as the educational definition of reflection frames it as a metacognitive practice where language learners revisit their own writing to correct gaps and deepen understanding, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow the structured, language-focused reflective routines from high school English writing workshops to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms. Specifically, requiring LLMs to walk through the same step-by-step reflection prompts students use to revise their work (identifying unclear logic, mismatched intentions, and logical gaps) can help reduce the inconsistency that current LLM self-reflection methods like Self-Contrast seek to address.
